executive_summary: Build workflow dispatch failed due to missing or inaccessible GitHub Actions workflow file. The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml could not be found in the repository, preventing any build execution. This is a critical configuration error that must be resolved before any Maven build can be attempted.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates that the workflow file does not exist at the expected path .github/workflows/build.yml or the GitHub token lacks necessary permissions to access it.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository at branch 'main', or the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) being used lacks the 'workflow' scope required to dispatch workflow events. The GitHub API documentation confirms this is a 404 Not Found error from the workflow dispatch endpoint.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No Maven build can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, testing, and deployment processes.

steps: 1. Create .github/workflows directory structure if it doesn't exist
2. Create build.yml workflow file with Maven build configuration
3. Configure workflow with workflow_dispatch trigger to enable manual/API triggering
4. Add Maven build steps: checkout code, setup JDK, run mvn clean install
5. Verify GitHub PAT has 'workflow' scope enabled
recommended_fix.workflow_template:
name: Maven Build
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      source_folder:
        description: 'Source folder containing Maven project'
        required: false
        default: 'code'
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - name: Build with Maven
        run: |
          cd ${{ github.event.inputs.source_folder || 'code' }}
          mvn clean install -B
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Workflow file creation requires repository write access. Ensure proper approval workflow for infrastructure changes. Document workflow configuration in compliance audit log.

errors.title: GitHub Actions May Be Disabled
errors.description: The 404 error could also indicate that GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository. This would prevent any workflow from being dispatched or executed.
errors.error_message: Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled.
errors.root_cause: GitHub Actions may be disabled at the repository or organization level, preventing workflow dispatch API calls from succeeding.
errors.impact: Cannot execute any CI/CD workflows until Actions is enabled. Blocks all automated build, test, and deployment processes.

steps: 1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Verify 'Actions permissions' is set to 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows' or appropriate setting
3. Ensure 'Workflow permissions' allows read and write permissions if needed
4. Check organization-level Actions policies if applicable
5. Re-attempt workflow dispatch after enabling Actions
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Enabling GitHub Actions may require organization admin approval. Document security review and approval in audit log.

errors.title: GitHub Token Missing Workflow Scope
errors.description: The GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) being used may lack the 'workflow' scope required to dispatch workflow events via the GitHub API.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. token missing workflow/actions permissions
errors.root_cause: The GitHub token used for API authentication does not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow_dispatch events.
errors.impact: Cannot programmatically trigger builds via API. Manual workflow triggers may still work, but automated CI/CD pipeline is blocked.

steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Locate the token being used (or create new token)
4. Also verify 'repo' scope is enabled for repository access
6. Re-attempt workflow dispatch with updated token
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Token scope changes require security review. Document token permissions and rotation schedule in compliance audit log. Follow principle of least privilege.

summary.build_stage_description: Error occurred before Maven build could start - workflow dispatch failed

next_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Create .github/workflows/build.yml workflow file with Maven build configuration
2. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub Actions is enabled for the repository
3. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub PAT has 'workflow' scope enabled
4. After fixes: Re-trigger build workflow dispatch
5. Monitor workflow execution and capture action_url for detailed log analysis
6. If workflow succeeds, proceed to code compilation and testing phases

compliance_audit_log.analysis_method: Automated error classification from build failure logs
compliance_audit_log.manual_review_reason: Configuration errors require human verification of repository settings and token permissions
compliance_audit_log.remediation_approval_reason: Creating workflow files and modifying repository configuration requires security review
